GSE303984.reasoning: The experiment is CITE-seq on PBMCs targeting T cell response to COVID-19 infection and vaccination, using a 10x 5' assay to read TCR, GEX, and ADT layers separated by GSM. | The samples belong to a CITE-seq experiment studying PBMC responses to COVID-19 vaccination/infection using 10x 5' chemistry, with individual GSMs manually separated into TCR and GEX layers as indicated by titles and library types. | Samples are unambiguously part of a split CITE-seq experiment based on sample titles ending in omic layer ('GEX') and the layer split hint grouping matching biological specimens. | The experiment utilizes CITE-seq (GEX, ADT surface proteins, and multiplexing barcodes) on PBMC collected from COVID-19 infected and previously vaccinated donors, where files are uploaded as split layers per matching biological sample. | Samples are from PBMC of individuals monitored for COVID-19 immune responses, utilizing split omics for 5' GEX and ADT/Cell Hashing libraries across physical cell pools (channels) representing mixed patient samples. | The data originates from a 10x Chromium 5' CITE-seq and scTCR-seq analysis of PBMCs from COVID-19 vaccinated/infected individuals, and the specific layers (GEX, TCR, ADT/Hashing) for each biological sample channel are explicitly split into independent GSM records.
